But if the loop `while(p3->zhishu >b&&p3->next )` terminates because `p3->next` is NULL, `p3` points to the last node. The logic `while(p3->zhishu >b&&p3->next )` stops when `p3->zhishu <= b` OR `p3->next` is NULL. If it stops because `p3->next` is NULL, `p3` is the last node. If `p3->zhishu > b`, we need to append. If `p3->zhishu == b`, we add. If `p3->zhishu < b`, we insert before. The code handles `<` and `==`. It has a separate check for `p3->next == NULL`. This structure is fragile.

Also, the insertion logic in multiply() does not correctly handle all cases for inserting or combining terms, leading to duplicate terms (e.g., two "400 500000" terms) instead of summing them.]
